prerequisites: A Recharge merchant account with store owner access (only store owners can create and view API tokens by default).
An API token created in the Recharge admin under Tools & apps > API tokens, with at least Read access to the resources you want to sync.
A start_date value (the earliest date from which to replicate records).

Decide whether to set lookback_window_days (optional, to re-fetch recently updated records) and use_orders_deprecated_api (optional, to read from the deprecated Orders API instead of the current one).

authMethods.name: API access token (X-Recharge-Access-Token)
authMethods.summary: Recharge uses API key authentication. The connector sends your token in the X-Recharge-Access-Token request header over HTTPS. Provide this value in the required, secret access_token field. Tokens are created in the Recharge admin and can be scoped to No access, Read access, or Read and Write access per resource.

setupSteps.title: Create a Recharge API token
setupSteps.body: Log into the Recharge merchant portal as a store owner. Go to Tools & apps > API tokens and click Create an API Token. Enter a name and contact email under Details, then set the permission level (at minimum Read access) for the resources you intend to sync, and click Save. Copy the generated token; it will be sent in the X-Recharge-Access-Token header.

setupSteps.title: Provide the access token
setupSteps.body: Enter the token you generated into the required access_token field. This is a secret credential and is used to authenticate every API request.

setupSteps.title: Set the replication start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which records should be replicated. Records created or updated before this date are not pulled on the initial sync.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional sync behavior
setupSteps.body: Optionally set lookback_window_days to re-read records updated within a trailing window on incremental syncs. Optionally enable use_orders_deprecated_api if you need data from Recharge's deprecated Orders API rather than the current orders endpoint. Leave both unset to use defaults.

sources.title: Recharge API token (creating an API token)
